build_from.reason: The Engine.Run/invokeAgent flow is the exact seam Epic 2.0 extends. Currently a single-shot completer.Complete(ctx, inv) call per agent (line 252); the tool loop wraps this in a turn loop that consumes tool_calls from the response, executes them locally, and re-invokes the completer with appended role:tool messages.
build_from.suggested_approach: Introduce a new AgentLoop struct (or extend invokeAgent) that, when an Agent has Tools==true, drives a multi-turn conversation. Add a ChatCompleter interface with Chat(ctx, inv, messages, tools) -> (message, tool_calls, error). llmclient.Client implements it; test fakes script tool_calls sequences. The existing Completer interface is preserved for non-tool agents and for the doctor package (internal/doctor/run.go:43) which only needs Complete.

existing_patterns.name: Completer interface
existing_patterns.description: fanout.Completer abstracts the LLM call (Complete(ctx, inv) -> string). Engine takes a Completer; production uses *llmclient.Client; tests use fakes. The doctor package has its own separate Completer (internal/doctor/run.go:43) that only needs Complete — no coupling to the new Chat method.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Add a ChatCompleter interface alongside Completer with Chat(ctx, inv, messages, tools) -> (message, tool_calls, error). llmclient.Client implements both; test fakes script tool_calls sequences. The doctor.Completer is unaffected — it never sees tool agents.

existing_patterns.name: Reserved registry fields
existing_patterns.description: AgentConfig already has Tools (bool, line 64), MaxTurns (*int, line 66), ToolBudgetBytes (*int64, line 67), Role (string, line 68) — parsed, validated, inert in 1.x. MaxAgentTurns constant (1000) bounds the turn budget and is defined in internal/registry/precedence.go:15.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Epic 2.0 activates these fields. No schema changes needed — just wire the engine to read AgentConfig.Tools/MaxTurns/ToolBudgetBytes and apply them. Add default application (max_turns=10 when tools=true and unset). The MaxAgentTurns=1000 ceiling in precedence.go already guards the upper bound.

existing_patterns.name: Slot/Agent with fallback chain
existing_patterns.description: Slot{Primary Agent, Fallbacks []Agent, Serial bool} (engine.go:39). Agent has Name, Invocation, Prompt, PayloadMode, Truncation, TimeoutSecs (engine.go:24). invokeSlot tries primary then fallbacks (engine.go:169).
existing_patterns.follow_for: Extend Agent struct with Tools bool, MaxTurns int, ToolBudgetBytes int64. When Tools is true, invokeAgent runs the tool loop instead of single-shot. Fallback inherits effective tools setting per epic spec. Result struct (engine.go:56) also needs Turns/ToolCalls/ToolBytes fields to propagate loop counters to the artifact layer.

existing_patterns.name: PayloadContext template rendering
existing_patterns.description: PayloadContext (template.go:15) is the typed data passed to persona prompt templates. RenderPrompt (template.go:70) parses and executes against it. Currently has AgentName, BaseRef, HeadRef, FileCount, PayloadMode, Payload, ScopeRule. The personas render test (personas_render_test.go:11) verifies every embedded persona renders cleanly against PayloadContext.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Add ToolsEnabled bool field to PayloadContext. Update RenderPrompt callers in review.go:438 to set it from AgentConfig.Tools. Update personas_render_test.go sample context with ToolsEnabled=false (existing personas render without tool sections). Add a test that {{if .ToolsEnabled}} conditionals work.

existing_patterns.name: Status/artifact layer
existing_patterns.description: AgentStatus (status.go:225) already has reserved *int ToolCalls (line 241), *int64 ToolBytes (line 242), *int Turns (line 240) fields (parsed+tested, inert). status.json is written per-agent via WriteStatus (status.go:249). statusFor() in artifacts.go:176 builds AgentStatus from a Result.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Epic 2.0 populates these fields during the tool loop. Add ToolsDegraded bool field to AgentStatus. Result struct gains Turns/ToolCalls/ToolBytes fields so statusFor() can propagate them. writeAgentArtifacts (artifacts.go:160) also writes transcript.jsonl alongside review.md.

existing_patterns.name: OpenAI wire format
existing_patterns.description: llmclient uses a minimal chatRequest/chatResponse struct with messages[{role,content}] (client.go:155-162). No tool_calls support yet. Complete (client.go:165) sends a single user message and returns the assistant content string.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Extend chatRequest with Tools []ToolDef (omitted when nil), chatResponse choices with Message.ToolCalls, and a new role:tool message type. Add Chat method alongside Complete. Keep backward compat — tools omitted when no tool agent. The attempt() method (client.go:243) parses the response and needs to handle tool_calls in choices.

existing_patterns.name: Embedded persona files
existing_patterns.description: Six embedded persona files (bruce.md, greta.md, kai.md, mira.md, dax.md, otto.md) plus _base.md under personas/. Accessed via personas.Get(name) and personas.Base(). These are Go embed files — any change requires re-compilation.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Add {{if .ToolsEnabled}} conditional sections to _base.md and each persona .md file. These sections contain tool-specific guidance: how to budget exploration, verify suspicions before reporting, prefer reading enclosing files over guessing, and the rule that findings must cite evidence actually read. The conditional keeps non-tool personas unchanged.

related_files.purpose: Core fanout engine — Run/invokeSlot/invokeAgent (line 228). This is where the tool loop gets inserted. Agent struct (line 24) and Result struct (line 56) need tool fields.

Review orchestration — PrepareReview, ExecuteReview, buildAgent (line 411), buildSlots (line 360). Propagate Tools/MaxTurns/ToolBudgetBytes from AgentConfig to Agent; pass ToolsEnabled to PayloadContext at prompt render time (line 438).
OpenAI-compatible HTTP client. Needs Chat method with tool_calls support in request/response wire format. chatRequest (line 155) and chatResponse (line 162) structs need extension.
AgentConfig (line 54) with reserved Tools/MaxTurns/ToolBudgetBytes/Role fields already parsed+validated. applyDefaults (line 209) needs activation for max_turns=10 when tools=true.
AgentStatus (line 225) with reserved ToolCalls/ToolBytes/Turns fields. Needs ToolsDegraded bool field.
PayloadContext struct (line 15) needs ToolsEnabled bool field. RenderPrompt (line 70) is the template engine that will render {{if .ToolsEnabled}} sections.
writeAgentArtifacts (line 160) writes review.md/findings.txt/status.json. Needs transcript.jsonl writer alongside review.md. statusFor() (line 176) propagates Result fields to AgentStatus.
Manifest struct (line 18). Stages field (line 35) already reserved for review stage. Needs richer stages entry listing tools:true agents.
Persona resolution chain (ResolvePersona, line 54). No changes to resolution logic — the {{if .ToolsEnabled}} support is in PayloadContext/template.go, not in persona resolution.
Registry documentation (line 54 has agent fields table). Needs to activate tools/max_turns/tool_budget_bytes from 'reserved' to 'active'.
Payload mode documentation. Needs section on payload-as-starting-point semantics for tool agents — tools widen evidence gathering, not scope.
MaxAgentTurns=1000 constant (line 15) bounds the turn budget. May need DefaultMaxTurns=10 constant for the tools=true default.
TestEmbeddedPersonasRenderAgainstContext (line 11) verifies all personas render against PayloadContext. Must update sample context when ToolsEnabled is added.
Unit tests for RenderPrompt. sampleCtx() (line 12) and TestRenderPrompt_AllVars need ToolsEnabled field.
Has its own Completer interface (line 43). Doctor only uses Complete, not Chat — no modification needed, but useful reference for interface segregation.
MCP server creates *llmclient.Client and passes it as fanout.Completer. Will automatically get Chat capability without code changes. Verify at integration time.

integration_points.description: The tool loop replaces the single completer.Complete call (line 252) when Agent.Tools is true. New code path: build messages from prompt, call ChatCompleter.Chat with tools, loop on tool_calls, append tool results, repeat until final message or budget trip. Result struct (line 56) gains Turns/ToolCalls/ToolBytes fields.

Add a Chat(ctx, inv, messages, tools) method alongside Complete. The wire format adds tools array to chatRequest and parses tool_calls from chatResponse choices. New message struct variant with ToolCalls and tool_call_id fields.
When building Agent from AgentConfig, propagate Tools/MaxTurns/ToolBudgetBytes to the Agent struct so the engine sees them. Also pass ToolsEnabled to PayloadContext at RenderPrompt call (line 438).

Fallback agents inherit the effective tools setting of the lane invocation. Propagate Tools/MaxTurns/ToolBudgetBytes from the primary's AgentConfig (the fallback's own config), not from the primary agent.

Write raw/<agent>/transcript.jsonl alongside review.md. Each turn (tool_calls + tool results + final message) is one JSONL line. statusFor() (line 176) propagates Result.Turns/ToolCalls/ToolBytes to AgentStatus.
Add ToolsEnabled bool field. This enables {{if .ToolsEnabled}} conditional sections in persona templates. Set by buildAgent when AgentConfig.Tools is true.
When tools==true and max_turns is nil, apply max_turns=10 default. This is the activation point for the reserved fields.

integration_gaps.description: Result struct (engine.go:56) lacks Turns/ToolCalls/ToolBytes fields — the tool loop cannot propagate counters to the artifact/status layer without them.
integration_gaps.resolution: Add Turns int, ToolCalls int, ToolBytes int64 fields to Result. Populate in the tool loop; consume in statusFor() and writeAgentArtifacts().

integration_gaps.description: PayloadContext (template.go:15) lacks ToolsEnabled bool — persona templates cannot conditionally render tool-specific guidance sections.
integration_gaps.resolution: Add ToolsEnabled bool field to PayloadContext. Set it in buildAgent (review.go:411) from AgentConfig.Tools before calling RenderPrompt.

integration_gaps.description: AgentStatus (status.go:225) lacks ToolsDegraded bool — when a non-tool-capable model has tools:true and degrades to single-shot, status.json has no field to record this.
integration_gaps.resolution: Add ToolsDegraded bool field to AgentStatus with json tag "tools_degraded,omitempty". Set true in the degrade path.

integration_gaps.description: Manifest Stages (manifest.go:35) only records []string{"review"} — no way to record which agents used tools in the review stage.
integration_gaps.resolution: Either extend Stages to a richer type (e.g., map[string]StageInfo) or add a separate ToolAgents []string field. The plan says 'stages.review entry with tools:true agents listed'.

integration_gaps.description: applyDefaults (config.go:209) does not apply max_turns=10 when tools=true — the reserved fields have no defaults in 1.x.
integration_gaps.resolution: In applyDefaults or in a new applyToolDefaults method, check a.Tools==true && a.MaxTurns==nil and set MaxTurns=&10. Similarly consider default tool_budget_bytes.

reusable_components.name: llmclient.Invocation
reusable_components.usage_example: Extend with optional Tools []ToolDef field for the Chat method. Existing Complete callers unaffected.

reusable_components.name: fanout.Completer interface
reusable_components.usage_example: Add a ChatCompleter interface (Chat method) alongside Completer. Engine checks for ChatCompleter via type assertion when Agent.Tools is true. Test fakes implement both for deterministic tool-loop tests. Doctor.Completer (internal/doctor/run.go:43) remains unaffected.

reusable_components.name: registry.AgentConfig reserved fields
reusable_components.usage_example: Already parsed+validated. Just wire engine to read them. Add default application for max_turns=10 when tools=true and unset.

reusable_components.name: fanout.AgentStatus counters
reusable_components.usage_example: ToolCalls/ToolBytes/Turns fields exist as *int/*int64 pointers (lines 240-242). Populate during tool loop execution. Add ToolsDegraded bool.

reusable_components.name: payload.PayloadContext
reusable_components.usage_example: Add ToolsEnabled bool field. RenderPrompt (line 70) and existing template engine handle it automatically via {{if .ToolsEnabled}} conditionals.

reusable_components.name: fanout.atomicWriteFile
reusable_components.usage_example: Use for writing transcript.jsonl — temp file + rename for crash safety. Already used by writeAgentArtifacts for review.md and status.json.

architecture_notes: The engine's Completer interface is the clean seam for adding tool calling. llmclient.Client implements Complete; add Chat alongside it. The Engine.invokeAgent method branches on Agent.Tools to choose single-shot vs. loop. A type assertion (Completer → ChatCompleter) avoids polluting the base interface.

Registry already validates Tools/MaxTurns/ToolBudgetBytes/Role at load (config.go:197-202). No schema migration needed — just activate defaults (max_turns=10 when tools=true) and wire the engine. MaxAgentTurns=1000 in precedence.go:15 already caps the upper bound.

The path jail and snapshot manager are new packages (internal/tools/jail.go, internal/tools/snapshot.go). They are consumed by the tool dispatcher, not the engine directly. The jail resolves paths relative to snapshot root; rejects absolute paths, .. escapes, out-of-root symlinks, .git/ access. The persona.go readNonEmpty function (line 130) already refuses symlinks — a similar security pattern for the path jail.

transcript.jsonl is a new artifact alongside review.md. The existing writeAgentArtifacts flow (artifacts.go:160) is the natural place to add it. Each JSONL line records one turn: role, content, tool_calls (if any), tool results (if any).

The tool loop must respect context cancellation and per-agent timeout (TimeoutSecs). The existing per-agent context.WithTimeout in invokeAgent (engine.go:237) already handles this — the tool loop inherits the same timeout.

Provider function-calling wire format: OpenAI's tools/tool_calls/role:tool is the lowest common denominator. litellm normalizes most providers to this format. The degrade path (tools:true on incapable model) falls back to single-shot with tools_degraded:true in status.json.

PayloadContext.ToolsEnabled is the template-level switch for persona conditional sections. The buildAgent function (review.go:411) sets it from AgentConfig.Tools before calling RenderPrompt. This keeps persona resolution (persona.go) unchanged — it only selects which text to use; the template engine handles the conditional rendering.

The doctor.Completer (internal/doctor/run.go:43) and MCP server (internal/mcp/server.go) both consume fanout.Completer but never invoke tool agents. Doctor probes are single-shot; MCP passes *llmclient.Client as Completer. No code changes needed in either — the Chat method is opt-in via ChatCompleter type assertion.

files_to_modify.reason: Extend invokeAgent (line 228) with tool loop when Agent.Tools is true; add Agent fields for Tools/MaxTurns/ToolBudgetBytes; add Turns/ToolCalls/ToolBytes to Result struct (line 56); add ChatCompleter interface alongside Completer.
Add Chat method with tools array in request, tool_calls in response parsing, role:tool message support. Extend chatRequest (line 155) and chatResponse (line 162) structs.
Add ToolsEnabled bool field to PayloadContext (line 15). This enables {{if .ToolsEnabled}} conditional sections in persona templates.

Propagate Tools/MaxTurns/ToolBudgetBytes from AgentConfig to Agent struct during slot building (buildAgent line 411). Set PayloadContext.ToolsEnabled at RenderPrompt call (line 438). Propagate in buildFallbackAgent (line 460).
Add default application for max_turns=10 when tools=true and unset in applyDefaults (line 209). Possibly add validation for tool_budget_bytes bounds.
Add DefaultMaxTurns=10 constant for the tools=true default. MaxAgentTurns=1000 (line 15) already exists.
Add ToolsDegraded bool field to AgentStatus (line 225). Populate ToolCalls/ToolBytes/Turns during tool loop execution.
Add transcript.jsonl writer in writeAgentArtifacts (line 160); update statusFor() (line 176) to propagate Turns/ToolCalls/ToolBytes from Result.
Add richer stages entry listing tools:true agents. Current Stages []string (line 35) only records ["review"].
Activate tools/max_turns/tool_budget_bytes documentation from 'reserved' to 'active' in agent fields table (line 54).
Add section on payload-as-starting-point semantics for tool agents — tools widen evidence gathering, not scope. File already exists with 67 lines of payload mode documentation.
Update sample PayloadContext with ToolsEnabled=false field (line 16) so existing persona render tests pass unchanged.
Update sampleCtx() (line 12) and TestRenderPrompt_AllVars with ToolsEnabled field. Add test for {{if .ToolsEnabled}} conditional rendering.

files_to_create.purpose: Tool dispatcher — receives tool_calls from model, routes to read_file/grep/list_files handlers, returns tool results.
Tool definitions (JSON Schema) for read_file, grep, list_files. OpenAI function-calling format.

Path jail — resolves paths relative to snapshot root, rejects absolute paths, .. escapes, out-of-root symlinks, .git/ access. Pattern: similar security posture to persona.go readNonEmpty (line 130) which already refuses symlinks.
Snapshot manager — live worktree fast path (head==HEAD && clean), git worktree add for other heads, cleanup after run.
transcript.jsonl writer — one JSON line per turn (tool_calls, tool results, final message).
Unit tests for tool dispatch, path jail, snapshot manager — no LLM involved.
httptest-scripted integration tests for Chat method with multi-turn tool_calls exchanges.
Integration tests for the tool loop — scripted mock provider, budget trips, degrade path.
